summary: Iteration 4 (multi-source job ingest: Adzuna ES+GB, Jooble, Remotive with content_hash dedupe) fully verified end-to-end. Backend: 12/12 pytest pass against live Adzuna + Jooble + Remotive APIs. Frontend: Jobs page renders, Pull fresh jobs button calls /api/jobs/ingest and displays a breakdown panel showing all 4 sources (e.g. '+50 new jobs from adzuna es (15/15), adzuna gb (15/15), jooble (15/15), remotive (5/15)'). 'live · adzuna' and 'live · remotive' source badges render on job cards with the correct selectors; 'live · jooble' uses the same code path and will render whenever a jooble job lands in the top-50 ranked list.
critical_code_review_comments: job_sources.py: clean implementation. _content_hash(SHA1 of title|company|location|source_url, lowercased) is deterministic and a sensible dedupe key. _insert_dedup correctly skips when content_hash exists; also drops rows with no source_url (good).

ingest_all() runs all sources sequentially. With 3 external HTTP calls (Adzuna x2 countries + Jooble + Remotive) at 20s timeout each, worst-case latency could be ~80s. Consider asyncio.gather() to parallelize — would cut p99 to ~20s. Not blocking, but worth a follow-up.

routes_jobs.py /api/jobs/ingest: error handling wraps the entire ingest_all() in a single try/except → 502. ingest_all() ALREADY captures per-source errors into the 'errors' dict and returns 200 with partial results. So the outer try/except only triggers for true catastrophic failures (e.g. DB down) — correct behaviour, just noting it.

models / db.jobs: there's NO unique index on content_hash. Dedupe is enforced application-side via the find_one check before insert_one. Under concurrent ingest requests this would race. Recommendation: add a unique partial index on {content_hash:1} (partial filter where content_hash exists) to make dedupe race-proof.

Adzuna pagination: fetch_adzuna() always hits page 1 (.../search/1). For query=python + limit=15 this is fine, but if a user requests >50 results the API just returns max 50 anyway. Not a bug given the limit=50 cap in /jobs/ingest route.
Older mock + remotive seed data (30 rows) predates the content_hash field. They won't dedupe against new ingests until they're re-ingested. Acceptable; not a bug.
Frontend Jobs.jsx: the source badge uses a generic `live · {source}` template and only suppresses when source==='mock'. Future-proof against new sources. Good.

Frontend breakdown panel renders Object.entries(by_source) — if the backend returns an empty by_source (all sources errored), the panel renders just '+0 new jobs from ' with nothing after. Minor cosmetic; not worth fixing unless seen in the wild.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iter4_multisource.py (NEW — 12 tests: multi-source ingest shape, dedupe, legacy mode, source field validation, real source_url assertion, content_hash persistence, env config, regression on /me/usage + /billing/me + /billing/cancel + /profile/upload-cv)
test_credentials: Bearer test_session_career_os / cookie session_token=test_session_career_os (user_testseed01). Pre-existing in DB.
seed_data_creation: Ran /api/jobs/ingest 3-4 times during testing. DB now contains 105 jobs: 53 adzuna, 12 jooble (initially) + 15 more after final pull, 30 remotive, 10 mock. All real-source jobs from iter4 carry content_hash. Old remotive+mock seed data (30 rows) does NOT carry content_hash but is harmless.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 4 multi-source ingest fully working. Live API rate limits to be aware of: Adzuna free is 1000 calls/month — each ingest_all() call burns 2 (one per country). Jooble is generous but still has per-key limits. If you re-run the full pytest suite plus a few manual ingests, expect ~10 Adzuna calls/run. Dedupe is verified via content_hash uniqueness (no dup rows in DB). The 'live · jooble' badge wasn't visible in the screenshot because jooble jobs ranked below the top 50 by skill match, but the badge component is source-agnostic and verified working for adzuna/remotive on the same code path. /api/billing/status iter2 bug is still open (carryover, not iter4 work).
rca_of_the_issue: No bugs found in iter4. Two initial test failures were test-strictness issues, not product bugs: (1) test_dedupe_second_run_inserts_zero asserted strict ==0 on 2nd run, but live Adzuna returns slightly different jobs between calls (1 new on second run for query='python', 4/5 deduped) — relaxed to assert 2nd-run total_inserted <= 1st AND assert zero duplicate content_hash rows exist in DB, which is the real correctness invariant. (2) test_content_hash_persisted picked an old pre-iter4 remotive seed job lacking the field — fixed to filter on source in {adzuna,jooble} which are iter4-era only, and additionally verified ALL 65 real-source iter4 jobs (53 adzuna + 12 jooble) have valid 40-char SHA1 content_hash.
